EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front moved through the region and the Mid Atlantic was located in the warm moist sector.  Convergence led to numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop in an atmosphere with high shear.  Heavy rain, damaging winds and tornadoes were observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A metal building structure in the south Locust Point Marine Terminal was damaged when a waterspout over the Patapsco River came onshore, transforming to a small tornado from approximately 3:44 to 3:45 PM EDT on June 10, 2013.||The metal building structure had a portion of its northeast roof peeled up, wrapping it back and folding it over the east-facing side of the building.  Additionally, a small trailer on wheels was demolished as it was propelled to the northwest about 60 feet from its position on Cinder Blocks.  An air conditioning unit which was permanently installed in the wall of an office on the north facing side of the building in proximity to where the roof damage occurred was blown-in.  A large amount of debris was seen plastered to a east-facing chain link fence on the north side of the parking lot.||Areas to the north Marine Terminal were also surveyed, and no further damage was found.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of shortwaves diving through a broad upper level trough brought rounds of showers and thunderstorms through the last several days of June. With a weakly capped atmosphere, a cold pool associated with two such upper level waves helped to spark two areas of convection in the afternoon on June 28th. There were numerous reports of wind damage across eastern Ohio, northern West Virginia, and western Pennsylvania. In addition, training thunderstorms produced several areas of flooding, including some flash flooding across several counties in western Pennsylvania and northern West Virginia.

A warm front moved through the region and the Mid Atlantic was located in the warm moist sector. Convergence led to numerous showers and thunderstorms to develop in an atmosphere with high shear. Heavy rain, damaging winds and tornadoes were observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a road closed due to flooding at Annapolis Road and Superior Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front stalled across the Mid Atlantic and low pressure formed and moved along it.  Southerly flow advected gulf moisture into the region and showers and thunderstorms easily formed.  Heavy rain, hail, damaging winds and tornadoes occurred throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was flooding at Norbeck Road and Gude Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure across the Southern Great Lakes with a associated cold front moved eastward through the Ohio Valley.  A surface trough was located along Potomac Highlands and dew points rose into the low 70s.  Showers and thunderstorms moved across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down at Spielman Road and Bakersville Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across the Potomac Highlands late in the evening. Showers and thunderstorms were able to tap into a strong low level jet and bring strong gusts to the surface.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a large tree limb down near the intersection of Beaver Creek Church Road and Beaver Creek Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front was approaching from the west. Ahead of it temperatures reached their convective temps and wind speeds increased. Thunderstorms formed along a lee trough.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a swift water rescue at Mcendree and Rover Mill Roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching cold front helped trigger strong thunderstorms across the Maryland Eastern Shore during the afternoon and evening of the 28th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man was struck and injured by lightning where he was standing on Doctor Jack Road in Conowingo. He was taken to Christiana Hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front stalled across the Mid Atlantic and low pressure formed and moved along it. Southerly flow advected gulf moisture into the region and showers and thunderstorms easily formed. Heavy rain, hail, damaging winds and tornadoes occurred throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching cold front helped trigger a line of strong to locally severe thunderstorms along the Eastern Shore during the late evening of the 20th. This front also ended the longest and most oppressive hot spell of the summer of 2013.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a couple of trees in Still Pond.
